title: Driving Test

jokes.text: David saying his expired license is 'fine' when asked if he has it
jokes.setup: Johnny asks if David has his license, implying he needs to see it
jokes.punchline: David misinterprets and says it's 'fine' without showing it
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - David's deflection and avoidance is perfectly in character, with tight economic writing

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Do you have your license?

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: It's fine.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: I didn't ask if it was fine, I asked if you have it.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Yes, and I'm telling you it's fine.

jokes.text: Alexis reveals 'it took him like, 16 tries to get it last time' with gleeful specificity
jokes.setup: Context that David struggles with driving tests
jokes.punchline: Alexis knows embarrassing details and shares them eagerly
jokes.explanation: Good sibling dynamic - Alexis's delight in sharing David's failures establishes their relationship and David's driving anxiety

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Oh my God, you guys, David failed his driving test. Again. He parallel parked into a storefront. A storefront!

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: And when the examiner told him he failed, he cried. Like, actually cried. It was so embarrassing.

jokes.text: Alexis gasping dramatically at David's expired license
jokes.setup: David's license being expired is treated as serious
jokes.punchline: Alexis's over-the-top shocked gasp
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective character beat showing Alexis's dramatic nature

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: *gasps dramatically*

jokes.text: David's increasingly desperate denials: 'It didn't take me 16 tries!'
jokes.setup: Alexis revealing embarrassing driving test history
jokes.punchline: David's protests make him seem more pathetic, not less
jokes.explanation: Standard cringe comedy - David's attempts to defend himself only make things worse

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: You failed your driving test four times.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I didn't fail. I was asked to leave.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: That's failing.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: It's not the same thing.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: You were banned from two DMVs.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Those were politically motivated.

jokes.text: The detailed recounting of David's multiple driving test failures
jokes.setup: David claims it didn't take 16 tries
jokes.punchline: The family systematically lists each failure in excruciating detail
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalating structure that builds humiliation methodically - great cringe comedy

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: It didn't take 16 tries.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Oh really? Because I have a list here. Try number one...

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: When you tried to fix the sink and flooded the entire back room.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Try number two: the time you thought you could rewire the electrical panel.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Try number three, four, five, six...

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Okay, I get it.

jokes.text: Moira's clinical diagnosis: 'crippling test anxiety'
jokes.setup: David's multiple test failures
jokes.punchline: Moira's overly formal, medical-sounding explanation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moira moment - her pretentious vocabulary applied to a mundane situation

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: David, darling, I've consulted with several medical professionals, and they've all come to the same conclusion. You are suffering from a severe case of incompetence-itis. It's a degenerative condition, I'm afraid, with no known cure.

jokes.text: Moira asking David to drive after just learning his license is expired
jokes.setup: The entire conversation was about David not being able to drive
jokes.punchline: Moira immediately asks him to drive her
jokes.explanation: Perfect irony - Moira wasn't listening and asks for exactly what was just established as impossible

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: David, darling, would you drive me to the store?

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Moira, we just spent the last twenty minutes talking about how David can't drive.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Yes, well, that was before I needed to go to the store.

jokes.text: Moira saying 'It's fine' about her wallet/license in the exact same deflective way David did
jokes.setup: Johnny asks Moira about her wallet, just like he asked David
jokes.punchline: Moira uses David's exact evasive phrase
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback that reveals the family's shared traits - like mother, like son. Excellent economy and timing.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Did you take my wallet?

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: I don't know what you're talking about.

jokes.text: David hoping to contract a deadly lung infection rather than do paperwork
jokes.setup: David looking at boxes of paperwork he needs to review
jokes.punchline: He'd rather get a fatal disease than do his job
jokes.explanation: Great dark humor that perfectly captures David's dramatic aversion to responsibility

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I would honestly rather contract a fatal disease than go through all of this paperwork.

jokes.text: Patrick's admission that he never looked at paperwork in his galleries
jokes.setup: David stressing about paperwork
jokes.punchline: Patrick casually reveals his own complete negligence
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan moment showing Patrick's casual relationship with responsibility

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Patrick, I've been going through all these files and I'm realizing we haven't filed our corporate taxes in three years. Three years!

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: Oh yeah, I know.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: You know? How do you know that so casually?

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: Well, I also haven't filed my personal taxes in five years, so...

jokes.text: Patrick's non-answer when David asks if it ever came together
jokes.setup: David asks if Patrick's neglect ever worked out
jokes.punchline: Patrick deflects with 'I think we're getting sidetracked'
jokes.explanation: Subtle avoidance humor - the deflection implies the answer is 'no'

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Did your neglect ever actually work out?

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: I think we're getting sidetracked.

jokes.text: David being 'the owner of a very sad business with one under-performing employee'
jokes.setup: Patrick says David is his own boss now
jokes.punchline: David reframes it as owning failure with Patrick as the bad employee
jokes.explanation: Excellent self-aware humor with a great insult embedded - very quotable

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Well, I prefer to think of it as owning my failures. And you're the bad employee.

jokes.text: Patrick's escalating time periods for 'stepping away' (15 minutes to 2 hours)
jokes.setup: Patrick suggests taking a short break when overwhelmed
jokes.punchline: The 'short break' grows longer and longer as he talks
jokes.explanation: Nice escalation that reveals Patrick's own procrastination tendencies

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: You know, when things get overwhelming, I find it helpful to take a short break. Maybe step away for five minutes, clear your head, get some fresh air.

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: Actually, make that ten minutes. Ten minutes is really the optimal time to reset mentally.

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: Or, you know, fifteen. Fifteen minutes allows for a proper walk, maybe grab a coffee.

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: A half hour, really. A solid thirty minutes is when you start to see real benefits.

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: Maybe take the rest of the day. I mean, why fight it?

jokes.text: David's flat rejection: 'Oh yeah, that's not gonna happen right now'
jokes.setup: Patrick's elaborate setup about finding a 'lucky person' to drive him
jokes.punchline: David's immediate, blunt shutdown
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan reaction - the economy and timing make it land well

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Patrick, you can't just ask someone to be your lucky person. That's not a thing.

jokes.text: Patrick saying he needs a valid driver's license to run errands
jokes.setup: Patrick offers to help David with errands
jokes.punchline: He reveals he can't do errands because he needs David to drive him first
jokes.explanation: Good circular logic joke showing Patrick's manipulative 'helpfulness'

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: I'd be happy to help you with your errands.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Really? That would be amazing.

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: Well, actually, I can't do your errands because I need you to drive me to the car rental place first.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: So you need me to drive you to rent a car so you can do errands for me?

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: Yes.

jokes.text: David's perceptive observation: 'Hmm, that's really perceptive' when Patrick says it's not a good time to ask
jokes.setup: Patrick finally acknowledging his bad timing
jokes.punchline: David sarcastically commends Patrick for this obvious realization
jokes.explanation: Sharp sarcastic response that shows David's wit while highlighting Patrick's obvious realization

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Wow, Patrick. What an incredible breakthrough. You've finally figured out that showing up to someone's event uninvited and unannounced might not be the best timing. Next, you'll discover that water is wet.

jokes.text: Alexis having licenses in seven different countries
jokes.setup: David wondering why he didn't ask Alexis first
jokes.punchline: Alexis casually mentions having licenses in seven countries
jokes.explanation: Perfect Alexis moment - casually dropping evidence of her worldly adventures

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I have licenses in seven countries.

jokes.text: Alexis having an 'F' Class license for transport trucks
jokes.setup: Alexis mentioning multiple licenses
jokes.punchline: She has a commercial trucking license because she 'had a lot of people to move'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation that hints at Alexis's mysterious past - the casual explanation makes it even funnier

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: She has a commercial trucking license because she 'had a lot of people to move'.

jokes.text: Alexis mentioning parallel parking in a burka
jokes.setup: David saying not everyone can flirt their way to a license
jokes.punchline: Alexis matter-of-factly mentions the practical challenges of driving in certain clothing
jokes.explanation: Another perfect Alexis moment - casually revealing more exotic experiences with great specificity

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Not everyone can flirt their way to a license.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Well, you try parallel parking in a bandage dress.

jokes.text: Alexis asking David to hold the wheel while she checks her phone
jokes.setup: David is already anxious about driving
jokes.punchline: Alexis casually endangers them both to check her phone
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy moment showing Alexis's casual relationship with danger

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Oh my God, David, I'm getting a text from the group chat.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Alexis, eyes on the road!

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I am looking at the road. I'm also looking at my phone. I'm very talented.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: That is not talent, that is a felony!

jokes.text: Alexis having a 7-year-old drive her around Mumbai
jokes.setup: David panicking about holding the wheel
jokes.punchline: Alexis reveals she once had a child drive her in a major city
jokes.explanation: Exceptional Alexis moment - the specificity and casual delivery make this a standout. Perfectly absurd while staying in character.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Oh my God, oh my God, oh my God.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: What? I've had a child drive me through Manhattan.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: What?

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Yeah, it was fine.

jokes.text: Stevie's sobbing interrupting Johnny and Moira's complaint about the faucet
jokes.setup: Johnny and Moira are focused on their minor plumbing issue
jokes.punchline: Stevie suddenly starts crying, revealing much bigger problems
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment - the contrast between their trivial concern and her crisis

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: It's just a minor plumbing issue. We'll get it fixed.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Yes, darling. Hardly catastrophic.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: A minor plumbing issue? MINOR?! Do you have any idea what's happening in my life right now?

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: I'm falling apart. Everything is falling apart.

jokes.text: Moira diagnosing it as 'boy troubles, no doubt'
jokes.setup: Stevie is crying about serious business problems
jokes.punchline: Moira assumes it must be romantic drama
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Moira's assumptions and disconnect from others' real problems

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: I'm having some serious business problems.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Oh, a man?

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: No, it's not about a man.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: A woman?

jokes.text: Moira's dramatic description of the dripping: 'the incessant tap, tap, tap... Oh, it's so irritating, Stevie'
jokes.setup: Simple plumbing issue being discussed
jokes.punchline: Moira treats minor drip like psychological torture with theatrical language
jokes.explanation: Solid Moira moment - her theatrical approach to mundane problems with dramatic vocabulary

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: A drip? A *drip*? Do you understand what that means? It's not just water, it's the slow, methodical erosion of my will to live. Each drop is like a tiny hammer striking my very soul.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Mom, it's a leaky faucet.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: A leaky faucet is what they used in medieval torture chambers, David. I'm calling a plumber. Actually, I'm calling a therapist. For the faucet.

jokes.text: Moira's immediate assumption: 'Oh, boy troubles, no doubt'
jokes.setup: Stevie breaking down emotionally about business stress
jokes.punchline: Moira reduces complex business crisis to simple romantic problem
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Moira's assumption reveals her worldview and dismissive attitude toward women's other concerns

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: I can't handle this. The stress is overwhelming. I don't know what to do.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Oh darling, it's quite simple really. You're just having commitment issues with your business.

jokes.text: Moira offering Stevie a pill to forget her problems for two days
jokes.setup: Stevie is having an emotional breakdown about her business
jokes.punchline: Moira's solution is pharmaceutical amnesia
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark Moira humor - her casual attitude toward prescription drugs as a solution

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Have you considered pharmaceutical amnesia? A little Ambien, a glass of pinot grigio, and you'll wake up tomorrow with absolutely no recollection of your business venture.

jokes.text: Johnny's wounded response: 'Well, I didn't realize I had a downtick, Moira'
jokes.setup: Moira using him as example of business failure
jokes.punchline: Johnny's hurt confusion at being casually used as cautionary tale
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat that shows Johnny's wounded pride and confusion at Moira's casual insult

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: And that's why you need a solid business plan. Take your father, for instance—

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: What? Why am I the cautionary tale?

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Because you're a perfect example of what happens when you don't prepare.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: I prepared. I prepared extensively.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Darling, you lost everything.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: That's... that's not fair.

jokes.text: Moira's offer to get Stevie 'a pill... going to make you forget every last one of those irrational thoughts for at least two days!'
jokes.setup: Stevie having emotional breakdown about business
jokes.punchline: Moira offers highly specific pharmaceutical solution with casual attitude toward drug duration
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy - Moira's casual attitude toward strong medication and the specific 'two days' detail makes it both absurd and concerning

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Oh, Stevie. I have just the thing. I have a Xanax that I was prescribed in 1987 for a commercial audition that never happened. It's bound to still have some kick to it.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Moira, I don't think that's—

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: The expiration date has long since passed, which I'm told only makes them stronger. You'll feel nothing for anywhere between four and seven years.

jokes.text: David asking the driving examiner 'people aren't thinking about you the way that you're thinking about you'
jokes.setup: Alexis tells David that nobody cares about his driving test
jokes.punchline: David asks what that cryptic statement means
jokes.explanation: Good philosophy-turned-comedy moment with David's genuine confusion at Alexis's wisdom

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Nobody cares about your driving test, David.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: What does that mean? That's a very cryptic statement.

jokes.text: Alexis saying she walks through life 'in really nice shoes'
jokes.setup: David accusing Alexis of skating through life
jokes.punchline: Alexis corrects him that she walks, then immediately undermines her point by mentioning expensive shoes
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - Alexis trying to be modest but immediately revealing her privilege. Perfect delivery.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: You just skate through life without any real responsibilities or consequences.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I don't skate, David. I walk.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I just bought these Gianvito Rossi heels for $1,200.

jokes.text: Johnny's 'temporary fix' being a sandwich bag secured with an elastic
jokes.setup: Johnny proudly announces he fixed the drip
jokes.punchline: His 'fix' is a plastic bag that will just collect the water
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag showing Johnny's well-meaning incompetence

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: I fixed the drip.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: You put a plastic bag under it.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: It collects the water.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: That's not fixing it, that's just... delaying the inevitable.

jokes.text: Johnny's business advice: 'when that idea comes, it's going to appear so obvious' followed immediately by Stevie's practical suggestion about charging full price
jokes.setup: Johnny promising obvious solutions will come
jokes.punchline: Stevie immediately provides obvious solution that Johnny dismisses
jokes.explanation: Good ironic structure where Johnny's promised 'obvious' solution appears immediately but he misses it

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Look, these things always work themselves out. Something will come up.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: You could just ask David for help.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: No, no, no. That's not going to work.

jokes.text: Johnny's pivot from first to 'second or third idea' being the keeper
jokes.setup: Stevie suggests charging full price to long-term guests
jokes.punchline: Johnny immediately backtracks and calls it maybe the second or third best idea
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Johnny's inability to admit when others have good ideas

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Well, maybe the second or third best idea you've ever had.

jokes.text: Roland describing the new pulp mill floors as so clean 'you can eat your lunch off them'
jokes.setup: Roland is enthusiastic about visiting the new pulp mill
jokes.punchline: He's excited about the floor cleanliness specifically
jokes.explanation: Character humor showing Roland's simple enthusiasms and Bob's pride in his work

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: I'm telling you, Bob, this pulp mill is state-of-the-art. Have you seen the floors? Spotless! Not a speck of dust anywhere.

dialogue.speaker: Bob
dialogue.line: Roland, it's a pulp mill.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: I know what it is, but when was the last time you saw a pulp mill with floors this clean? It's a game-changer.

jokes.text: The driving examiner calling David 'Derek'
jokes.setup: David is nervous about making a good impression
jokes.punchline: The examiner doesn't even know his name
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing David's anxiety is completely one-sided

dialogue.speaker: Examiner
dialogue.line: Name?

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: David Rose.

dialogue.speaker: Examiner
dialogue.line: Okay... and what brings you in today?

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I'm here for my driver's license exam.

dialogue.speaker: Examiner
dialogue.line: Right. Well, let's get started then, um...

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: David. I just told you my name.

dialogue.speaker: Examiner
dialogue.line: Yes, yes. Of course.

jokes.text: David asking if the examiner cares about him 'in the car' after learning he's done eight tests today
jokes.setup: David realizes the examiner is just going through the motions
jokes.punchline: David's desperate attempt to make a personal connection
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing David's need for validation even in mundane situations

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Wait, wait, wait. Are you even listening to me? I'm pouring my heart out here.

dialogue.speaker: Examiner
dialogue.line: I'm listening. I'm just... filling out forms.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Well, could you fill out forms and make eye contact? I'm very vulnerable right now.

dialogue.speaker: Examiner
dialogue.line: I'm looking at you.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: No, you're looking *past* me. There's a difference. One is soul-crushing.

jokes.text: Moira's confession: 'You didn't get the money, did you?' followed by elaborate excuse about robbing Johnny of necessity
jokes.setup: Johnny asking about council funding success
jokes.punchline: Moira failed but creates elaborate philosophical justification for why failure is actually better
jokes.explanation: Excellent Moira moment - her elaborate rationalization for failure is perfectly in character and philosophically absurd

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: So how did it go with the council? Did you get the funding?

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, not exactly. But I've been thinking about it, and failure is actually just success that hasn't learned to present itself properly.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: In fact, I would argue that my rejection today is merely the universe's way of redirecting my considerable talents toward a more spiritually aligned endeavor.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: The funding was never the point, Johnny. The point was the journey of self-discovery that comes with public humiliation.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: So you didn't get it.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: I did not get it.

jokes.text: David's revelation about being constantly worried about Alexis being held hostage in 'East Asian palaces'
jokes.setup: David explaining why he doesn't trust Alexis
jokes.punchline: The specific mention of 'East Asian palaces' as a recurring concern
jokes.explanation: Exceptional moment - the specificity of 'East Asian palaces' is perfectly absurd while revealing genuine sibling dynamic. Highly quotable.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: You can't trust her. Last year she told me she was going to a yoga retreat in Sedona, and instead she went to East Asian palaces.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: It was ONE time, David.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: It was multiple palaces, Alexis.

jokes.text: Ray advising against partnering with someone with 'no capital, no contacts, no industry experience'
jokes.setup: Johnny offers to partner with Stevie
jokes.punchline: Ray accurately describes Johnny's complete lack of qualifications
jokes.explanation: Good ironic moment where the antagonist is actually giving sound advice

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: I think we should go into business together.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Okay.

dialogue.speaker: Ray
dialogue.line: Well, I would advise against that. Mr. Rose has no business experience, no financial acumen, no practical skills of any kind, no relevant education, no references, and a crippling lack of confidence in even the simplest tasks.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Well, I think that's a bit of an exaggeration.

dialogue.speaker: Ray
dialogue.line: Is it?

jokes.text: Johnny's 'fixed' tap now making the hot water come out of the cold tap
jokes.setup: Johnny proudly shows off his plumbing repair
jokes.punchline: The fix created a dangerous new problem - scalding water from the wrong tap
jokes.explanation: Good escalation callback - Johnny's help makes things worse, typical of his character

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Well, I fixed it. The leak is completely sealed.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Dad, why is scalding water coming out of the cold tap?

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: That's... that's a separate issue.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: So you've essentially created a booby trap in our only bathroom.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: I'll fix it.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: When? After you fix the other thing you fixed?

jokes.text: The tap can't be turned off and is burning Alexis's hand
jokes.setup: The hot water is coming out wrong
jokes.punchline: Now they can't turn it off and someone's getting burned
jokes.explanation: Good physical escalation that caps off Johnny's disastrous repair job

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: The hot water is coming out wrong.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Well, have you tried turning it off?

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Now they can't turn it off and someone's getting burned.

jokes.text: Johnny's proud declaration 'See? No drip' while chaos ensues around broken faucet
jokes.setup: Faucet shooting burning water that won't turn off
jokes.punchline: Johnny focuses on solving original minor problem while creating major new ones
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment - Johnny's tunnel vision and misplaced pride while everything goes wrong around him

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Well, the faucet is shooting water, it's on fire, and I can't turn it off.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: But if I can just get this temperature valve to work, everything will be fine.

tmdb_overview: Johnny and Moira worry about the future of the motel and David panics at the thought of retaking his driving test.
insight: Character escalation drives 42 jokes in 42 minutes—Moira's driving test audition peaks at 83.1.
